timingInterpretationPolicy.workloadUnitWallMetricUse: timed workload-unit wall end-to-end ranking metric
timingInterpretationPolicy.guidance: When timingInterpretation.selectedTiming.scopeClass is narrow-hot-path, deltaPercent remains a phase-specific diagnostic. Claimability evaluates timingInterpretation.workloadUnitWall.deltaPercent when that full metric is available.

workloads.name: matrix-vector multiply 32768x2048 f32 (naive swizzle0)
workloads.description: Matrix-vector compute kernel aligned to Dawn MatrixVectorMultiplyPerf dimensions (Rows=32768, Cols=2048, F32/F32, Impl=Naive, Swizzle=0, 100 dispatches).
workloads.comparabilityNotes: Apples-to-apples matrix-vector contract aligned to Dawn MatrixVectorMultiplyPerf variant geometry and per-dispatch normalization.

timingNormalization.note: Strict Dawn-vs-Doe operation comparability uses direct timing (left/right divisors fixed to 1.0).

selectedTiming.note: Selected timing scope could not be classified reliably from the timing metadata.

workloadUnitWall.note: Uses timed command process wall normalized by commandRepeat and timingNormalizationDivisor. This is the full timed workload-unit view for one comparable workload unit, not a warm-session-only metric.

selectedGap.note: The normalized difference between workloadUnitWall and selected timing for each timed sample.

attributedHostOverhead.note: Sum of coarse host-overhead buckets recorded outside the selected execution timing. These buckets are once-per-sample phase timers, not per-dispatch probes.

unattributedGapRemainder.note: Selected-gap remainder after subtracting the coarse host-overhead buckets. This typically captures trace-meta emission, process teardown, and any uninstrumented sample overhead.

inputRead.note: Input/config file reads before selected execution timing begins.

inputParse.note: Command/plan parsing and input decoding before selected execution timing begins.

workloadPrepare.note: Pre-execution workload preparation such as dispatch-context or buffer-spec setup.

executorInit.note: Executor/device/backend initialization outside the selected execution timing.

uploadPrewarm.note: Doe upload-path prewarm work outside the selected execution timing.

kernelPrewarm.note: Kernel/pipeline prewarm work outside the selected execution timing.

commandOrchestration.note: Command-loop bookkeeping outside traced execution phase timing.

artifactFinalize.note: Post-execution artifact writing/finalization outside the selected execution timing.

hostOverheadBreakdown.note: Coarse host-overhead buckets are recorded around existing once-per-sample phase boundaries so they explain workloadUnitWall minus selected timing without inserting hot-path profiling probes.

headlineProcessWall.note: Uses timed command process wall normalized by commandRepeat and timingNormalizationDivisor. This is the full timed workload-unit view for one comparable workload unit, not a warm-session-only metric.

selectedTiming.note: Selected timing scope could not be classified reliably from the timing metadata.

workloadUnitWall.note: Uses timed command process wall normalized by commandRepeat and timingNormalizationDivisor. This is the full timed workload-unit view for one comparable workload unit, not a warm-session-only metric.

selectedGap.note: The normalized difference between workloadUnitWall and selected timing for each timed sample.

attributedHostOverhead.note: Sum of coarse host-overhead buckets recorded outside the selected execution timing. These buckets are once-per-sample phase timers, not per-dispatch probes.

unattributedGapRemainder.note: Selected-gap remainder after subtracting the coarse host-overhead buckets. This typically captures trace-meta emission, process teardown, and any uninstrumented sample overhead.

inputRead.note: Input/config file reads before selected execution timing begins.

inputParse.note: Command/plan parsing and input decoding before selected execution timing begins.

workloadPrepare.note: Pre-execution workload preparation such as dispatch-context or buffer-spec setup.

executorInit.note: Executor/device/backend initialization outside the selected execution timing.

uploadPrewarm.note: Doe upload-path prewarm work outside the selected execution timing.

kernelPrewarm.note: Kernel/pipeline prewarm work outside the selected execution timing.

commandOrchestration.note: Command-loop bookkeeping outside traced execution phase timing.

artifactFinalize.note: Post-execution artifact writing/finalization outside the selected execution timing.

hostOverheadBreakdown.note: Coarse host-overhead buckets are recorded around existing once-per-sample phase boundaries so they explain workloadUnitWall minus selected timing without inserting hot-path profiling probes.

headlineProcessWall.note: Uses timed command process wall normalized by commandRepeat and timingNormalizationDivisor. This is the full timed workload-unit view for one comparable workload unit, not a warm-session-only metric.

selectedTiming.note: Selected timing scope could not be classified reliably from the timing metadata.

workloadUnitWall.note: Uses timed command process wall normalized by commandRepeat and timingNormalizationDivisor. This is the full timed workload-unit view for one comparable workload unit, not a warm-session-only metric.

selectedGap.note: The normalized difference between workloadUnitWall and selected timing for each timed sample.

attributedHostOverhead.note: Sum of coarse host-overhead buckets recorded outside the selected execution timing. These buckets are once-per-sample phase timers, not per-dispatch probes.

unattributedGapRemainder.note: Selected-gap remainder after subtracting the coarse host-overhead buckets. This typically captures trace-meta emission, process teardown, and any uninstrumented sample overhead.

inputRead.note: Input/config file reads before selected execution timing begins.

inputParse.note: Command/plan parsing and input decoding before selected execution timing begins.

workloadPrepare.note: Pre-execution workload preparation such as dispatch-context or buffer-spec setup.

executorInit.note: Executor/device/backend initialization outside the selected execution timing.

uploadPrewarm.note: Doe upload-path prewarm work outside the selected execution timing.

kernelPrewarm.note: Kernel/pipeline prewarm work outside the selected execution timing.

commandOrchestration.note: Command-loop bookkeeping outside traced execution phase timing.

artifactFinalize.note: Post-execution artifact writing/finalization outside the selected execution timing.

hostOverheadBreakdown.note: Coarse host-overhead buckets are recorded around existing once-per-sample phase boundaries so they explain workloadUnitWall minus selected timing without inserting hot-path profiling probes.

headlineProcessWall.note: Uses timed command process wall normalized by commandRepeat and timingNormalizationDivisor. This is the full timed workload-unit view for one comparable workload unit, not a warm-session-only metric.

workloads.name: workgroup-atomic 1024
workloads.description: Workgroup-atomic compute kernel with 1024 workgroups, 100 dispatches per step, matching Dawn WorkgroupAtomicPerf exactly.
workloads.comparabilityNotes: Kernel matches Dawn GenWorkgroupWithAtomics (256 threads, 1 atomicStore + 256 atomicLoads per invocation). Both sides dispatch 100 times per step with direct timing (no side-specific divisor scaling).

timingNormalization.note: Strict Dawn-vs-Doe operation comparability uses direct timing (left/right divisors fixed to 1.0).

selectedTiming.note: Selected timing scope could not be classified reliably from the timing metadata.

workloadUnitWall.note: Uses timed command process wall normalized by commandRepeat and timingNormalizationDivisor. This is the full timed workload-unit view for one comparable workload unit, not a warm-session-only metric.

selectedGap.note: The normalized difference between workloadUnitWall and selected timing for each timed sample.

attributedHostOverhead.note: Sum of coarse host-overhead buckets recorded outside the selected execution timing. These buckets are once-per-sample phase timers, not per-dispatch probes.

unattributedGapRemainder.note: Selected-gap remainder after subtracting the coarse host-overhead buckets. This typically captures trace-meta emission, process teardown, and any uninstrumented sample overhead.

inputRead.note: Input/config file reads before selected execution timing begins.

inputParse.note: Command/plan parsing and input decoding before selected execution timing begins.

workloadPrepare.note: Pre-execution workload preparation such as dispatch-context or buffer-spec setup.

executorInit.note: Executor/device/backend initialization outside the selected execution timing.

uploadPrewarm.note: Doe upload-path prewarm work outside the selected execution timing.

kernelPrewarm.note: Kernel/pipeline prewarm work outside the selected execution timing.

commandOrchestration.note: Command-loop bookkeeping outside traced execution phase timing.

artifactFinalize.note: Post-execution artifact writing/finalization outside the selected execution timing.

hostOverheadBreakdown.note: Coarse host-overhead buckets are recorded around existing once-per-sample phase boundaries so they explain workloadUnitWall minus selected timing without inserting hot-path profiling probes.

headlineProcessWall.note: Uses timed command process wall normalized by commandRepeat and timingNormalizationDivisor. This is the full timed workload-unit view for one comparable workload unit, not a warm-session-only metric.

workloads.name: workgroup-non-atomic 1024
workloads.description: Non-atomic workgroup compute kernel with 1024 workgroups, 100 dispatches per step, matching Dawn WorkgroupAtomicPerf non-atomic variant.
workloads.comparabilityNotes: Kernel matches Dawn GenWorkgroupNoAtomics (256 threads, 1 store + 256 loads per invocation). Both sides dispatch 100 times per step with direct timing (no side-specific divisor scaling).

timingNormalization.note: Strict Dawn-vs-Doe operation comparability uses direct timing (left/right divisors fixed to 1.0).

selectedTiming.note: Selected timing scope could not be classified reliably from the timing metadata.

workloadUnitWall.note: Uses timed command process wall normalized by commandRepeat and timingNormalizationDivisor. This is the full timed workload-unit view for one comparable workload unit, not a warm-session-only metric.

selectedGap.note: The normalized difference between workloadUnitWall and selected timing for each timed sample.

attributedHostOverhead.note: Sum of coarse host-overhead buckets recorded outside the selected execution timing. These buckets are once-per-sample phase timers, not per-dispatch probes.

unattributedGapRemainder.note: Selected-gap remainder after subtracting the coarse host-overhead buckets. This typically captures trace-meta emission, process teardown, and any uninstrumented sample overhead.

inputRead.note: Input/config file reads before selected execution timing begins.

inputParse.note: Command/plan parsing and input decoding before selected execution timing begins.

workloadPrepare.note: Pre-execution workload preparation such as dispatch-context or buffer-spec setup.

executorInit.note: Executor/device/backend initialization outside the selected execution timing.

uploadPrewarm.note: Doe upload-path prewarm work outside the selected execution timing.

kernelPrewarm.note: Kernel/pipeline prewarm work outside the selected execution timing.

commandOrchestration.note: Command-loop bookkeeping outside traced execution phase timing.

artifactFinalize.note: Post-execution artifact writing/finalization outside the selected execution timing.

hostOverheadBreakdown.note: Coarse host-overhead buckets are recorded around existing once-per-sample phase boundaries so they explain workloadUnitWall minus selected timing without inserting hot-path profiling probes.

headlineProcessWall.note: Uses timed command process wall normalized by commandRepeat and timingNormalizationDivisor. This is the full timed workload-unit view for one comparable workload unit, not a warm-session-only metric.

workloads.name: shader-compile pipeline stress
workloads.description: Shader robustness pipeline stress aligned with Dawn ShaderRobustnessPerf (MatMulFloatOneDimSharedArray, f32, 512x512x512).
workloads.comparabilityNotes: Matched compute pipeline creation + dispatch shape against Dawn ShaderRobustnessPerf MatMulFloatOneDimSharedArray f32 variant, including 50-dispatch step normalization.

timingNormalization.note: Strict Dawn-vs-Doe operation comparability uses direct timing (left/right divisors fixed to 1.0).

selectedTiming.note: Selected timing scope could not be classified reliably from the timing metadata.

workloadUnitWall.note: Uses timed command process wall normalized by commandRepeat and timingNormalizationDivisor. This is the full timed workload-unit view for one comparable workload unit, not a warm-session-only metric.

selectedGap.note: The normalized difference between workloadUnitWall and selected timing for each timed sample.

attributedHostOverhead.note: Sum of coarse host-overhead buckets recorded outside the selected execution timing. These buckets are once-per-sample phase timers, not per-dispatch probes.

unattributedGapRemainder.note: Selected-gap remainder after subtracting the coarse host-overhead buckets. This typically captures trace-meta emission, process teardown, and any uninstrumented sample overhead.

inputRead.note: Input/config file reads before selected execution timing begins.

inputParse.note: Command/plan parsing and input decoding before selected execution timing begins.

workloadPrepare.note: Pre-execution workload preparation such as dispatch-context or buffer-spec setup.

executorInit.note: Executor/device/backend initialization outside the selected execution timing.

uploadPrewarm.note: Doe upload-path prewarm work outside the selected execution timing.

kernelPrewarm.note: Kernel/pipeline prewarm work outside the selected execution timing.

commandOrchestration.note: Command-loop bookkeeping outside traced execution phase timing.

artifactFinalize.note: Post-execution artifact writing/finalization outside the selected execution timing.

hostOverheadBreakdown.note: Coarse host-overhead buckets are recorded around existing once-per-sample phase boundaries so they explain workloadUnitWall minus selected timing without inserting hot-path profiling probes.

headlineProcessWall.note: Uses timed command process wall normalized by commandRepeat and timingNormalizationDivisor. This is the full timed workload-unit view for one comparable workload unit, not a warm-session-only metric.

workloads.name: buffer-upload 1KB
workloads.description: Single upload command used to align with Dawn BufferUploadPerf WriteBuffer 1KB.
workloads.comparabilityNotes: Matched single queue write upload shape on both sides; Fawn uses deferred queue sync to avoid per-command wait inflation at tiny payload size while preserving per-upload timing normalization.

timingNormalization.note: Strict Dawn-vs-Doe operation comparability uses direct timing (left/right divisors fixed to 1.0).

workloads.pathAsymmetryNote: Doe upload may resolve to host-visible CPU writes without an equivalent GPU copy, while Dawn performs staging+copy. This hardware-path-specific advantage is non-transferable and non-claimable until structural equivalence is restored.

selectedTiming.note: Selected timing scope could not be classified reliably from the timing metadata.

workloadUnitWall.note: Uses timed command process wall normalized by commandRepeat and timingNormalizationDivisor. This is the full timed workload-unit view for one comparable workload unit, not a warm-session-only metric.

selectedGap.note: The normalized difference between workloadUnitWall and selected timing for each timed sample.

attributedHostOverhead.note: Sum of coarse host-overhead buckets recorded outside the selected execution timing. These buckets are once-per-sample phase timers, not per-dispatch probes.

unattributedGapRemainder.note: Selected-gap remainder after subtracting the coarse host-overhead buckets. This typically captures trace-meta emission, process teardown, and any uninstrumented sample overhead.

inputRead.note: Input/config file reads before selected execution timing begins.

inputParse.note: Command/plan parsing and input decoding before selected execution timing begins.

workloadPrepare.note: Pre-execution workload preparation such as dispatch-context or buffer-spec setup.

executorInit.note: Executor/device/backend initialization outside the selected execution timing.

uploadPrewarm.note: Doe upload-path prewarm work outside the selected execution timing.

kernelPrewarm.note: Kernel/pipeline prewarm work outside the selected execution timing.

commandOrchestration.note: Command-loop bookkeeping outside traced execution phase timing.

artifactFinalize.note: Post-execution artifact writing/finalization outside the selected execution timing.

hostOverheadBreakdown.note: Coarse host-overhead buckets are recorded around existing once-per-sample phase boundaries so they explain workloadUnitWall minus selected timing without inserting hot-path profiling probes.

headlineProcessWall.note: Uses timed command process wall normalized by commandRepeat and timingNormalizationDivisor. This is the full timed workload-unit view for one comparable workload unit, not a warm-session-only metric.

details.workloadPathAsymmetryNote: Doe upload may resolve to host-visible CPU writes without an equivalent GPU copy, while Dawn performs staging+copy. This hardware-path-specific advantage is non-transferable and non-claimable until structural equivalence is restored.

comparability.workloadPathAsymmetryNote: Doe upload may resolve to host-visible CPU writes without an equivalent GPU copy, while Dawn performs staging+copy. This hardware-path-specific advantage is non-transferable and non-claimable until structural equivalence is restored.

selectedTiming.note: Selected timing scope could not be classified reliably from the timing metadata.

workloadUnitWall.note: Uses timed command process wall normalized by commandRepeat and timingNormalizationDivisor. This is the full timed workload-unit view for one comparable workload unit, not a warm-session-only metric.

selectedGap.note: The normalized difference between workloadUnitWall and selected timing for each timed sample.

attributedHostOverhead.note: Sum of coarse host-overhead buckets recorded outside the selected execution timing. These buckets are once-per-sample phase timers, not per-dispatch probes.

unattributedGapRemainder.note: Selected-gap remainder after subtracting the coarse host-overhead buckets. This typically captures trace-meta emission, process teardown, and any uninstrumented sample overhead.

inputRead.note: Input/config file reads before selected execution timing begins.

inputParse.note: Command/plan parsing and input decoding before selected execution timing begins.

workloadPrepare.note: Pre-execution workload preparation such as dispatch-context or buffer-spec setup.

executorInit.note: Executor/device/backend initialization outside the selected execution timing.

uploadPrewarm.note: Doe upload-path prewarm work outside the selected execution timing.

kernelPrewarm.note: Kernel/pipeline prewarm work outside the selected execution timing.

commandOrchestration.note: Command-loop bookkeeping outside traced execution phase timing.

artifactFinalize.note: Post-execution artifact writing/finalization outside the selected execution timing.

hostOverheadBreakdown.note: Coarse host-overhead buckets are recorded around existing once-per-sample phase boundaries so they explain workloadUnitWall minus selected timing without inserting hot-path profiling probes.

headlineProcessWall.note: Uses timed command process wall normalized by commandRepeat and timingNormalizationDivisor. This is the full timed workload-unit view for one comparable workload unit, not a warm-session-only metric.

details.workloadPathAsymmetryNote: Doe upload may resolve to host-visible CPU writes without an equivalent GPU copy, while Dawn performs staging+copy. This hardware-path-specific advantage is non-transferable and non-claimable until structural equivalence is restored.

comparability.workloadPathAsymmetryNote: Doe upload may resolve to host-visible CPU writes without an equivalent GPU copy, while Dawn performs staging+copy. This hardware-path-specific advantage is non-transferable and non-claimable until structural equivalence is restored.

workloads.name: buffer-upload 4MB
workloads.description: Single upload command aligned with Dawn BufferUploadPerf WriteBuffer 4MB.
workloads.comparabilityNotes: Matched single queue write upload shape on both sides.

timingNormalization.note: Strict Dawn-vs-Doe operation comparability uses direct timing (left/right divisors fixed to 1.0).

workloads.pathAsymmetryNote: Doe upload may resolve to host-visible CPU writes without an equivalent GPU copy, while Dawn performs staging+copy. This hardware-path-specific advantage is non-transferable and non-claimable until structural equivalence is restored.

selectedTiming.note: Selected timing scope could not be classified reliably from the timing metadata.

workloadUnitWall.note: Uses timed command process wall normalized by commandRepeat and timingNormalizationDivisor. This is the full timed workload-unit view for one comparable workload unit, not a warm-session-only metric.

selectedGap.note: The normalized difference between workloadUnitWall and selected timing for each timed sample.

attributedHostOverhead.note: Sum of coarse host-overhead buckets recorded outside the selected execution timing. These buckets are once-per-sample phase timers, not per-dispatch probes.

unattributedGapRemainder.note: Selected-gap remainder after subtracting the coarse host-overhead buckets. This typically captures trace-meta emission, process teardown, and any uninstrumented sample overhead.

inputRead.note: Input/config file reads before selected execution timing begins.

inputParse.note: Command/plan parsing and input decoding before selected execution timing begins.

workloadPrepare.note: Pre-execution workload preparation such as dispatch-context or buffer-spec setup.

executorInit.note: Executor/device/backend initialization outside the selected execution timing.

uploadPrewarm.note: Doe upload-path prewarm work outside the selected execution timing.

kernelPrewarm.note: Kernel/pipeline prewarm work outside the selected execution timing.

commandOrchestration.note: Command-loop bookkeeping outside traced execution phase timing.

artifactFinalize.note: Post-execution artifact writing/finalization outside the selected execution timing.

hostOverheadBreakdown.note: Coarse host-overhead buckets are recorded around existing once-per-sample phase boundaries so they explain workloadUnitWall minus selected timing without inserting hot-path profiling probes.

headlineProcessWall.note: Uses timed command process wall normalized by commandRepeat and timingNormalizationDivisor. This is the full timed workload-unit view for one comparable workload unit, not a warm-session-only metric.

details.workloadPathAsymmetryNote: Doe upload may resolve to host-visible CPU writes without an equivalent GPU copy, while Dawn performs staging+copy. This hardware-path-specific advantage is non-transferable and non-claimable until structural equivalence is restored.

comparability.workloadPathAsymmetryNote: Doe upload may resolve to host-visible CPU writes without an equivalent GPU copy, while Dawn performs staging+copy. This hardware-path-specific advantage is non-transferable and non-claimable until structural equivalence is restored.

workloads.name: buffer-upload 64KB
workloads.description: Single upload command aligned with Dawn BufferUploadPerf WriteBuffer 64KB.
workloads.comparabilityNotes: Matched single queue write upload shape on both sides.

timingNormalization.note: Strict Dawn-vs-Doe operation comparability uses direct timing (left/right divisors fixed to 1.0).

selectedTiming.note: Selected timing scope could not be classified reliably from the timing metadata.

workloadUnitWall.note: Uses timed command process wall normalized by commandRepeat and timingNormalizationDivisor. This is the full timed workload-unit view for one comparable workload unit, not a warm-session-only metric.

selectedGap.note: The normalized difference between workloadUnitWall and selected timing for each timed sample.

attributedHostOverhead.note: Sum of coarse host-overhead buckets recorded outside the selected execution timing. These buckets are once-per-sample phase timers, not per-dispatch probes.

unattributedGapRemainder.note: Selected-gap remainder after subtracting the coarse host-overhead buckets. This typically captures trace-meta emission, process teardown, and any uninstrumented sample overhead.

inputRead.note: Input/config file reads before selected execution timing begins.

inputParse.note: Command/plan parsing and input decoding before selected execution timing begins.

workloadPrepare.note: Pre-execution workload preparation such as dispatch-context or buffer-spec setup.

executorInit.note: Executor/device/backend initialization outside the selected execution timing.

uploadPrewarm.note: Doe upload-path prewarm work outside the selected execution timing.

kernelPrewarm.note: Kernel/pipeline prewarm work outside the selected execution timing.

commandOrchestration.note: Command-loop bookkeeping outside traced execution phase timing.

artifactFinalize.note: Post-execution artifact writing/finalization outside the selected execution timing.

hostOverheadBreakdown.note: Coarse host-overhead buckets are recorded around existing once-per-sample phase boundaries so they explain workloadUnitWall minus selected timing without inserting hot-path profiling probes.

headlineProcessWall.note: Uses timed command process wall normalized by commandRepeat and timingNormalizationDivisor. This is the full timed workload-unit view for one comparable workload unit, not a warm-session-only metric.

details.workloadPathAsymmetryNote: Doe upload may resolve to host-visible CPU writes without an equivalent GPU copy, while Dawn performs staging+copy. This hardware-path-specific advantage is non-transferable and non-claimable until structural equivalence is restored.

comparability.workloadPathAsymmetryNote: Doe upload may resolve to host-visible CPU writes without an equivalent GPU copy, while Dawn performs staging+copy. This hardware-path-specific advantage is non-transferable and non-claimable until structural equivalence is restored.

workloads.name: concurrent execution single-dispatch contract
workloads.description: Compute contract aligned to Dawn ConcurrentExecutionTest RunSingle with a single heavy compute dispatch.
workloads.comparabilityNotes: Apples-to-apples contract: command shape and Dawn filter mapping both exercise one long-running compute dispatch per benchmark step.

selectedTiming.note: Selected timing scope could not be classified reliably from the timing metadata.

workloadUnitWall.note: Uses timed command process wall normalized by commandRepeat and timingNormalizationDivisor. This is the full timed workload-unit view for one comparable workload unit, not a warm-session-only metric.

selectedGap.note: The normalized difference between workloadUnitWall and selected timing for each timed sample.

attributedHostOverhead.note: Sum of coarse host-overhead buckets recorded outside the selected execution timing. These buckets are once-per-sample phase timers, not per-dispatch probes.

unattributedGapRemainder.note: Selected-gap remainder after subtracting the coarse host-overhead buckets. This typically captures trace-meta emission, process teardown, and any uninstrumented sample overhead.

inputRead.note: Input/config file reads before selected execution timing begins.

inputParse.note: Command/plan parsing and input decoding before selected execution timing begins.

workloadPrepare.note: Pre-execution workload preparation such as dispatch-context or buffer-spec setup.

executorInit.note: Executor/device/backend initialization outside the selected execution timing.

uploadPrewarm.note: Doe upload-path prewarm work outside the selected execution timing.

kernelPrewarm.note: Kernel/pipeline prewarm work outside the selected execution timing.

commandOrchestration.note: Command-loop bookkeeping outside traced execution phase timing.

artifactFinalize.note: Post-execution artifact writing/finalization outside the selected execution timing.

hostOverheadBreakdown.note: Coarse host-overhead buckets are recorded around existing once-per-sample phase boundaries so they explain workloadUnitWall minus selected timing without inserting hot-path profiling probes.

headlineProcessWall.note: Uses timed command process wall normalized by commandRepeat and timingNormalizationDivisor. This is the full timed workload-unit view for one comparable workload unit, not a warm-session-only metric.
